notes: Smallest possible round-trip: one user prompt, one assistant reply, no tool use, asserting the ready→working→ready arc with TotalTokens > 0 after the turn. Recorded with the HEADLESS pi driver (`pi --print -p <prompt>`): pi runs the single turn and exits. Pi writes one JSONL session file per invocation on launch (FilesUnderRoot, non-lazy), so the daemon sees a proc-<PID> pre-session born ready, then the rollout session reconcile (new session_id) born ready, then the one working→ready flip as the turn runs, then process_exited at headless exit. first_turn_end anchors to first_turn_start (not session_birth) so the matcher picks the post-turn ready, not the earlier birth ready — pi's stopReason=stop on the final assistant event maps to turn_done. The TotalTokens > 0 assertion the scenario validates isn't expressible in the phase DSL today (no metric form in the invariant DSL); it's covered by the pi parser unit tests.

text: Pre-session appears in ready within 1s of launch
Rollout session appears (different session_id), born ready
PID bound to the rollout session
User prompt → working on the rollout session
Assistant reply completes → back to ready; state never entered waiting
Headless pi exits after the single turn — process_exited on the rollout session
